mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations collapse.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon through improved corporate revenue visibility.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during INDPRO contractions in late-cycle regimes, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL growth above 2.8% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.8 threshold, or the signal shows zero predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending power and supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no positive correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a synchronized demand shock, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as recession fears mount.
failure_condition: The negative divergence signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns in over 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60% of post-shock recovery periods where INDPRO accelerates, or the signal loses predictive significance in walk-forward validation.
